title: 2 Terminology and Frequently Asked Questions
body_text:
2	Terminology and Frequently Asked Questions
2.1	Terminology
2-Step Security
2-step security is an extra layer of authentication thatГўВҖВҷs added to an application. To log in to an application that is protected by 2-step security, a user must enter the one-time password from their registered 2-step security token.
2-Step Security Token
A 2-step security token is a device or application that generates a random one-time password thatГўВҖВҷs recognized by applications that are protected by 2-step security.
Business User
A business user is your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs business customer who has access to a limited set of Central 1ГўВҖВҷs applications (e.g.,
PaymentStreamГўВ„Вў
AFT). Business users donГўВҖВҷt have access to the Client Centre. The procedure for managing business users is the same as those for Client Centre Users, unless otherwise noted.
Client Centre
The Client Centre is a digital portal that provides secured access to Central 1ГўВҖВҷs applications and to information and news about our products and services.
Client Centre User
Client Centre Users log in to the Client Centre to access certain applications. Client Centre Users canГўВҖВҷt manage Security Officers (User Management / CPS Admin Security Officers) or other Client Centre Users.
CPS Admin
The Central Portal System (CPS) Admin application is the legacy application used to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users and Security Officers.
Unlisted Transit Number
An unlisted transit number is a 9-digit number in the format of a standard transit number (e.g.,  080912345). Business customers need an unlisted transit number for Corporate Capture, while branches that donГўВҖВҷt have a transit number need one for Branch Capture. Unlisted transit numbers arenГўВҖВҷt issued by or registered with Payments Canada. Dishonoured items will never be returned to these numbers.
User Management Security Officer
User Management Security Officers use the User Management or CPS Admin application to add, modify, and delete Client Centre Users and to modify and delete other User Management Security Officers. User Management Security Officers are also referred to as CPS Admin Security Officers. User Management Security Officers use the Client Centre to log in to certain applications.
Hard Token
A hard token is a physical 2-step security token.
Soft Token
A soft token is a 2-step security token thatГўВҖВҷs installed on the userГўВҖВҷs mobile device as a mobile app.
User Management
The User Management application is used to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users. ItГўВҖВҷs also used to edit and delete other Security Officers and to manage 2-step security tokens.
2.2	Frequently Asked Questions
General Client Centre and User Management Questions
Question 1
Do I need access to User Management?
Answer
You only need access to User Management if youГўВҖВҷre a designated User Management Security Officer (Security Officer) for your financial institution. Security Officers are authorized to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users, to manage other Security Officers, and to manage 2-step security tokens.
Question 2
Who are my Security Officers?
Answer
Contact Client Support Services to help identify your Security Officers. See Section 1.2, Contacts, Websites, and Other Resources.
Question 3
How do I become a Security Officer?
Answer
See Section 4.2, Adding a User Management Security Officer, for procedures.
Question 4
IГўВҖВҷm a Security Officer and I created a new user, but the user is having trouble logging in to the Client Centre. What should I do?
Answer
See Chapter 12, Troubleshooting and Best Practices for Client Centre Users.
Question 5
IГўВҖВҷm a Security Officer and I reset a userГўВҖВҷs password. How long does the user have to wait to use the new password?
Answer
Wait up to 15 minutes for the new password to take effect.
2-Step Security Token Management
Question 6
What soft token authenticator do you recommend?
Answer
Google Authenticator; however, any authenticator is fine.
Question 7
How do I order a hard token?
Answer
Security officers order hard tokens using the ГўВҖВңOrder Hard TokensГўВҖВқ request in ServiceNow. Tokens are shipped directly from the supplier. See the 2-Step Security Part for more information.
Question 8
When a userГўВҖВҷs 2-step security token is unlocked, does the user have to wait before attempting to log in again?
Answer
No, thereГўВҖВҷs no delay associated with unlocking a 2-step security token.
Question 9
If a user gets a new phone, is a new token required?
Answer
Yes. Deactivate the old token, then assign a new one.
END
